data.title: Citrea: Peter Thiel's Key Deployment on the Bitcoin L2 Network
data.description:
Author: Ryan Yoon, Source: Tiger Research, Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • Most Bitcoin L2 security solutions don't actually utilize Bitcoin's security mechanisms. Citrea achieves this through zero-knowledge proofs and BitVM, allowing Bitcoin to directly verify its state.

  • Founders Fund has a strong track record of discovering unique technologies (e.g., SpaceX and Palantir). Choosing Citrea when Bitcoin L2 prices were at their lowest point demonstrates their unwavering belief in the technology.

  • Founders Fund has a proven track record of discovering unique technologies (e.g., SpaceX and Palantir). Their choice of Citrea when Bitcoin L2 prices were at their lowest point also reflects their strong belief in the technology.

    • The Citrea team doesn't have any particularly illustrious credentials. However, they were the first to validate zero-knowledge proof technology in a Bitcoin production environment and launched the mainnet before most Bitcoin secondary payment systems had even gone live. The technology validation is complete. The remaining challenge is to give EVM ecosystem users a reason to use the same services on Bitcoin. The next step is to actively expand the user base. 1. L2 Skepticism and Citrea: Why Thiel Chose the latter Bitcoin's market capitalization has surpassed $2 trillion. However, when asked about the actual use of this money, the answer is unclear. "Make this money work. Utilize Bitcoin's security." This is the core logic behind the surge in Bitcoin L2 prices. But of the dozens of projects born from this logic, very few have actually been effective. The need was clear, but the results were less than satisfactory. Against this backdrop, Peter Thiel's Founders Fund invested in Citrea, choosing a team capable of building the first zero-knowledge base aggregation on Bitcoin, rather than another marketing gimmick. This was a high-conviction bet on the forefront of Bitcoin's L2 technology, made precisely at a time when the overall market was most skeptical. 2. Why Thiel chose Citrea? Founders Fund has a history of investing in unique technologies that other companies dare not venture into. Limited partners have called the investment in SpaceX "crazy." One partner at Kleiner Perkins even walked out midway through a Palantir roadshow. The investment in Citrea follows the same technological conviction. While most companies in the Bitcoin L2 space are focused on marketing over-engineered sidechains, Citrea focuses on solving the "from zero to one" problem, truly bringing zero-knowledge technology to Bitcoin. Founders Fund stepped in as market confidence in Bitcoin L2 plummeted. Partner Joey Krug believes Citrea represents "the strongest team and technical architecture in the Bitcoin L2 space." As the company's first investment in the Bitcoin ecosystem, this move is not about following trends; it's about supporting the only design that can ultimately make Bitcoin's $2 trillion capital a programmable design. 2.1. A Team That Proved Its Zero-Knowledge Capabilities Before developing based on Bitcoin, the team released a "Proof of Innocence." In 2022, the U.S. Treasury Department sanctioned Tornado Cash for money laundering. The collateral damage was immediate. Innocent users with no connection to the illegal activities were also treated the same way. The team utilizes zero-knowledge proofs, allowing users to prove their wallets are not associated with sanctioned addresses without revealing their identity. RAILGUN DAO integrates this protocol. Vitalik Buterin has also publicly cited it. None of the founders come from large tech companies or well-known protocol fields. Yet, they have successfully launched a zero-knowledge sharing product that is already in production and has received external validation. Founders Fund's assessment of them as a "best team" is not unfounded.

    2.2. Solving the Core Problem of Bitcoin L2 with Zero-Knowledge Proofs

    Citrea overcomes the core obstacle of Bitcoin L2 using zero-knowledge proof technology, leveraging Bitcoin's actual security to run services on it.

    Most Bitcoin L2 security mechanisms assign security permissions to a small group of signers. This can be imagined as a vault shared by five key holders, where any three can authorize withdrawals. This model is quick and practical, but it doesn't fully inherit Bitcoin's security because it relies on the integrity of a few people. Most Bitcoin Level 2 (L2) security layers are not significantly different in structure. Design details may vary, but the reliance on a few signers remains consistent across the domain. Citrea built a 1/N system where only one honest participant is needed. For example, when a user attempts to withdraw Bitcoin from Citrea, the process is controlled by four roles: Signer: Cryptographically locks the withdrawal conditions before any request is made. Once set, the conditions cannot be changed, preventing operators from manipulating withdrawal operations. Operator: Can only process withdrawals within the limits set by the signer. Collateral must be provided in advance, and cryptographic proof of its validity must be submitted to retrieve it. **Watchtower:** Simultaneously monitors Citrea and Bitcoin. If the operator attempts a fraudulent withdrawal, the Watchtower collects evidence and passes it to the challenger. **Challenger:** Uses the evidence provided by Watchtower to invalidate the fraudulent withdrawal on Bitcoin. Once fraud is confirmed, the operator's collateral is forfeited. The system can continue to operate if any one of these four participants remains honest. However, there is a challenge: Bitcoin's base layer is not designed for complex computations. How can cryptographic proof verification and dispute resolution mechanisms operate on top of it? ZK technology and BitVM make this possible. They can implement cryptographic proof verification and dispute resolution directly on Bitcoin. Founders Fund is optimistic about this unique technology. 3. How Citrea Actually Works The following is an application of the above architecture in practice. The system is driven by three components.

    1. ZK Rollup:Compresses transaction data and publishes it to Bitcoin

    2. BitVM:Supports optimistic Bitcoin native verification

    3. Clementine:Citrea's Bitcoin bridge based on BitVM

    3.1. ZK Proof: Thousands of transactions, one proof

    Take a notary office as an example. Notarizing thousands of contracts one by one is time-consuming and expensive. A lawyer (Citrea) only needs to review all the contracts and submit a notarized document proving that "all these contracts are valid."

    The notary office (Bitcoin) only needs to review this one document. It's quick, cheap, and the final notarization record is still kept by the notary office. This certification is a zero-knowledge proof. Citrea processes thousands of transactions off-chain, generates zero-knowledge proofs using zkEVM, and records them on the Bitcoin underlying layer. Anyone running Citrea and a Bitcoin node can verify whether Citrea's state is correct. 3.2. BitVM: No Rule Changes Required One problem remains. Bitcoin was not designed to verify complex zero-knowledge proofs. Its scripting language can only handle basic transaction conditions. Running complex calculations directly on Bitcoin is virtually impossible. Citrea solves this problem using BitVM. You can think of it as a notary office's appeal window. Under normal circumstances, zero-knowledge proofs are accepted according to their submission status. If someone questions the validity of a proof, Bitcoin intervenes and verifies it directly. The computation only runs when a dispute arises; it does not run by default. Bitcoin's consensus rules remain unchanged. 3.3. **Clementine: One Honest Actor Is Enough** In any Bitcoin secondary payment system, the bridge is the weakest link. If funds are stolen during a transfer between Bitcoin and Citrea, the entire architecture fails. Citrea's bridge, Clementine, takes a different approach. This security model requires only one honest participant out of N participants. If the operator attempts fraud, the challenger uses the chainhead proof issued by Watchtower to initiate a challenge transaction directly on the Bitcoin network, forfeiting the operator's collateral. Funds cannot be stolen unless all participants are compromised simultaneously. Standard multi-signature bridges transfer funds only when two out of three people agree, while Clementine ensures fund security as long as even one honest participant is present. The result is that attacking Citrea is tantamount to attacking Bitcoin itself. This requires controlling more than half of the total computing power to block challenges during the disputed window, which is fundamentally different from threatening a small group of signers. 4. The Current State of Citrea Most projects conduct token generation (TGE) before launching the mainnet. Testnets allow failures without consequences. This model is common: sell the vision first, then postpone the technology launch. But the mainnet is different. It involves real funds, real transactions, and real accountability. Citrea did the opposite. The mainnet launched before the token generation exchange (TGE), along with cBTC (bridged to Citrea via BTC) and ctUSD (Citrea's native stablecoin), with over 40 applications already running at launch. Applications such as Satsuma, Signals, Zentra, Crest, and CellFi all belong to the Citrea Origins project, which is directly supported by the Citrea team. Unlike most Bitcoin Level 2 (L2) cryptocurrencies that focus on staking and yield, Citrea directly supports developers in the DEX, prediction market, money market, privacy trading, and payments sectors. The intention is clear: to expand the practical functionality of Bitcoin L2 and build genuine usability. Despite this, Citrea is still in its early stages. The TVL is approximately $6 million, and while the testnet event attracted 33,000 participants, this momentum has not yet carried over to the mainnet, even after the launch of the new mainnet event. The network has been online for less than three months, and key services, including the Morpho Vault, were only recently introduced. Citrea's long-term vision is to become a hub for Bitcoin applications, building an ecosystem that goes far beyond staking and yielding. The goal is clear, but user acquisition is still underway. 5. Design vs. Reality Web3 is rife with projects peddling ideas: decentralization, censorship resistance, financial sovereignty. These ideas sound easy, but actually getting funding is difficult. Citrea's technical solution is sound. Bitcoin L2 should truly utilize Bitcoin's security mechanisms. Two trillion dollars of idle funds. Zero-knowledge proofs are the only way to achieve this. The Founders Fund agrees, and Citrea has successfully launched its mainnet. This distinguishes it from projects that only talk the talk. Next comes a more thorny issue. Most Bitcoin holders are not uneasy. Holding Bitcoin is perfectly normal. The idea of ​​"building on Bitcoin" doesn't appeal to ordinary users. Secure infrastructure is a necessary, but not sufficient, condition. What Citrea needs now is not more technical proofs, but rather to make the operations users perform on Arbitrum and Base work better on Bitcoin. This is precisely the mission of the Origins project (decentralized exchanges, money markets, privacy trading, prediction markets). If these applications cannot attract users, then the infrastructure will remain just infrastructure. The foundation is laid, the highway is built, and the traffic lights are installed. Now all that's left is to wait for the cars. No matter how well the road is paved, without traffic, it's just an empty asphalt road. The next challenge for Citrea isn't building a better road, but finding drivers.

data.title: Internal strife erupts at decentralized AI project Bittensor: Covenant AI team withdraws.
data.description: The capital market's faith in "decentralized AI" (DeAI) is facing an unprecedented stress test. Recently, Bittensor ($TAO), the undisputed leader in the decentralized AI field, suffered a devastating internal earthquake. Covenant AI, one of the top development teams in the Bittensor ecosystem, which had just successfully trained a 72-byte language model, suddenly announced its complete withdrawal from the Bittensor network via social media. In its departure statement, Covenant AI directly criticized Bittensor founder Jacob Steeves, accusing him of having "absolute and dictatorial" control over the network, arbitrarily cutting off token rewards for subnets, and stating that the so-called decentralized AI was nothing more than a carefully orchestrated "drama." Affected by this black swan event, the price of the $TAO token experienced a panic sell-off in the secondary market, with a single-day drop of 15% to 25%, instantly wiping out hundreds of millions of dollars in market value. While the crypto community was observing the public break between the top team and the founder, it also began to seriously examine a deeper industry question: In the AI ​​field, which heavily relies on computing power and complex engineering, is the "decentralization" driven by token economics a utopia that reshapes production relations, or merely a glamorous cloak to conceal centralized power? To understand the destructive power of this incident, we must first recognize the significance of Covenant AI within the Bittensor ecosystem. In Bittensor's multi-subnet architecture, most subnets are still at the low-level API call, model fine-tuning, or simple task routing stages. Teams truly capable of training from scratch or large-scale parametric models are extremely rare. Covenant AI is precisely the "hardcore" representative of this ecosystem. Shortly before announcing their withdrawal, the team delivered a milestone to the community: successfully training a massive open-source model with 72 billion parameters (72B) in a decentralized network environment. At current computing power costs, training a 72B model requires mobilizing a massive GPU cluster (essentially equivalent to thousands of H100 GPUs running continuously for weeks) and incurring extremely high hardware and electricity costs. Covenant AI's willingness to bear such huge upfront sunk costs stemmed from Bittensor's "Emissions" mechanism—as long as its provided models and computing power achieved high scores in subnet evaluations, it would continuously receive $TAO token releases as substantial rewards. This is precisely the most attractive flywheel effect in the DeAI narrative. However, the flywheel came to an abrupt halt at its climax. According to Covenant AI's disclosure, after investing heavily in training and launching its 72B model, founder Jacob Steeves and his stakeholders, by controlling the validators, directly cut off the token rewards flowing to the Covenant AI subnet without any warning or transparent governance process. For miners and developers, cutting off Emissions is tantamount to "pulling the plug." The ROI of huge computing power expenditures instantly became zero. This extremely unpredictable systemic risk directly triggered Covenant AI's angry exit. The word "charade" used by Covenant AI in its exit statement precisely struck Bittensor's most vulnerable nerve: network control. Bittensor's underlying design relies on the Yuma consensus, the core of which is that "validators" evaluate the contributions of "miners" and determine how the newly issued $TAO tokens are distributed. Theoretically, this is a decentralized game system based on staking and algorithms. However, Covenant AI's accusations reveal a harsh reality: **computing power is decentralized, but power and capital are highly concentrated.** In the current Bittensor root network, the top validator nodes that dominate token distribution have their staking highly concentrated in the addresses associated with early investors, the foundation, and founder Jacob Steeves. This means the founder is not only the rule-maker but also the ultimate referee. Covenant AI points out that when a subnet's output does not align with Jacob's personal wishes or may threaten the interests of other "direct descendant" subnets, Jacob can easily use his massive staking weight to alter the Yuma consensus distribution. This "one-man show" intervention renders the decentralization at the smart contract level meaningless. Developers spend millions of dollars on computing power, yet their fate ultimately depends on the subjective will or behind-the-scenes manipulation of a single founder. Objectively speaking, Jacob and his supporters might defend this by citing reasons such as "maintaining the overall quality of the network" and "preventing specific subnets from exploiting loopholes to farm coins." However, in the absence of a transparent DAO governance mechanism, on-chain hearings, and appeal channels, this centralized intervention, acting as a "benevolent authority," severely undermines the network's core value as a "trustworthy and neutral infrastructure." The 15-25% single-day plunge in $TAO is not merely a panic sell-off by retail investors, but rather a repricing of Bittensor's "governance risk discount" by institutional investors. Bittensor's massive market capitalization and high valuation premium stem from the market's perception of it as the only realistic target for "decentralized OpenAI." The foundation of this grand narrative is that the system must possess extremely high predictability: as long as you contribute computing power and a high-quality model, the protocol will automatically guarantee your returns through code. The Covenant AI incident shattered this expectation. Top financial professionals and institutional investors abhor "unpredictable single-node failures," and in this case, the point of failure was Jacob Steeves' power. If even a top-tier team capable of training a 72B model can instantly lose everything due to the founder's intervention, then for other computing power providers and AI research institutions holding tokens and waiting to see what happens, deploying heavy assets on Bittensor is undoubtedly a Russian roulette game where the table could be overturned at any moment. When high-quality supply-side resources (miners and developers) refuse to enter due to fear of centralized tyranny, the application scenarios and intrinsic value of the $TAO token become unsustainable. The frantic exodus of funds is a preemptive vote against this deteriorating fundamental situation. The departure of Covenant AI is not merely a public relations crisis for Bittensor, but an inevitable growing pain for the entire decentralized AI field as it enters deeper waters. It brutally reveals the "impossible triangle" of DeAI: model quality and scale, decentralized trust neutrality, and incentive alignment to prevent malicious behavior. Centralization of Scale vs. Decentralization of Mechanisms: Training cutting-edge AI (such as large models exceeding 72 bytes) is a typical capital-intensive, centralized project requiring highly collaborative GPU clusters. This presents a natural physical gap with the permissionless, distributed nodes advocated by Web3. **Anti-Fraudulent Traffic vs. Trusted Neutrality:** To prevent low-quality nodes from fraudulently obtaining tokens through mutual traffic manipulation (a Sybil attack), the network must introduce subjective "quality assessment." However, with AI assessment standards not yet fully objective and mathematically standardized, entrusting this assessment power to a few validators can easily degenerate into centralized rent-seeking. Bittensor attempted to bridge this gap with token economics, but the Covenant incident proved that the foundation of this bridge (governance mechanism) remains extremely fragile. The departure of Covenant AI punctured the romantic bubble of Bittensor's "absolute decentralization." For $TAO, this may be a painful moment of demystification, but for the entire DeAI industry, it serves as a necessary wake-up call.

data.title: Goldman Sachs plans to launch a BTC premium ETF; Tether launches a self-custodied wallet.
data.description:
Headlines

▌Goldman Sachs Plans to Launch a "Bitcoin Premium Yield ETF"

On April 14th, according to market news, Goldman Sachs has submitted an application to launch a "Bitcoin Premium Yield ETF".

▌Tether Launches Self-Custodial Digital Wallet tether.wallet

On April 14th, according to official news, Tether announced the launch of tether.wallet, a self-custodial digital wallet that puts Tether's global financial infrastructure directly in the hands of users. With the launch of tether.wallet, this funds distribution network is directly open to end users for the first time.

... As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $74,191.84, a 24-hour change of -0.6%; ETH price is $2,325.68, a 24-hour change of -2.1%; BNB price is $614.25, a 24-hour change of -0.3%. SOL price is $83.95, a 24-hour change of -2.9%; DOGE price is $0.09308, a 24-hour change of -1.2%; XRP price is $1.36, a 24-hour change of -1.0%; TRX price is $0.3237, a 24-hour change of +0.8%. The price of WLFI is $0.08066, with a 24-hour change of -1.6%; the price of HYPE is $43.47, with a 24-hour change of -1.9%. Policy: ▌US Senators plan to release draft amendments to stablecoin yields this week, potentially impacting market structure legislation. On April 14th, US Senator Thom Tillis stated that the Senate is expected to release the text of a draft amendment regarding stablecoin yield distribution this week. Currently, banks and crypto companies disagree on whether to allow cryptocurrency exchanges to pay stablecoin yields to stablecoin holders through reward programs, a dispute that has stalled the Clarity Act legislation process. A recent report from the White House Council of Economic Advisers stated that banning stablecoin yields would have a minimal impact on small banks, increasing lending by only 0.02%. However, the American Banking Association believes this analysis underestimates the risks. Observers point out that if the draft regulations are too strict, it could lead to a flow of users and liquidity to other jurisdictions that allow yields.

▌People's Daily criticizes "100x coins" and other practices that use false profit information to attract traffic

On April 14, People's Daily published an article titled "Disrupting the Capital Market: These Irregularities in Financial Self-Media Accounts Need to Be Wary Of," specifically naming some accounts that use false profit information such as "100x coins" and "making millions a month trading cryptocurrencies" to attract traffic to virtual currency trading.

The article again cites a previous notice from eight departments including the People's Bank of China, emphasizing that virtual currency-related business activities are illegal financial activities, and points out that accounts and platforms providing traffic and technical services for illegal virtual currency transactions are suspected of violating laws and regulations, and may threaten financial security and anti-money laundering efforts.

▌Foreign Ministry: If the US imposes tariffs on China under the pretext of Iran, China will resolutely retaliate

On April 14, Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun hosted a regular press conference. A reporter asked, "Recently, some media reported that China is providing military support to Iran, and US President Trump stated that if it is found that China is providing weapons to Iran, the US will impose a 50% tariff on Chinese goods. What is China's comment on this?" Guo Jiakun said, "We have already stated China's position. We would like to reiterate that China has always adopted a prudent and responsible attitude towards military exports, and implements strict controls in accordance with its own export control laws and regulations and its international obligations. The relevant reports are pure fabrication.

... "If the US insists on using this as a pretext to impose additional tariffs on China, China will resolutely retaliate," said Guo Jiakun. (Beijing Daily) The US State Department issued a statement on April 15 following a trilateral meeting between Israel, Lebanon, and the US in Washington. The statement noted that the meeting was chaired by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio. The participants had a "productive" discussion on various measures to promote the initiation of direct negotiations between Israel and Lebanon. The US statement emphasized that any ceasefire agreement must be reached by both governments under US mediation. The US also pointed out that the negotiations are expected to promote Lebanon's reconstruction and economic recovery, and expand regional investment opportunities. Israel expressed its support for dismantling all non-state armed forces in Lebanon and removing related "terrorist infrastructure," and pledged to resolve differences through direct negotiations to achieve lasting peace and regional stability. Lebanon reiterated its commitment to the full implementation of the November 2024 ceasefire declaration, emphasizing national sovereignty and territorial integrity, and calling for concrete measures to alleviate the severe humanitarian crisis caused by the ongoing conflict. All parties agreed to formally launch direct negotiations at a time and place agreed upon by Israel and Lebanon. (CCTV News)

Blockchain Applications

▌Ethereum Foundation Launches Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program

On April 14, the Ethereum Foundation launched the Ethereum Audit Subsidy Program, a collaborative project with audit service providers aimed at subsidizing the cost of security audits for Ethereum developers. Security audits are industry best practices, but are expensive. The goal of this subsidy program is to make audits more affordable, thereby enhancing the security of the entire Ethereum ecosystem.

▌NVIDIA Releases Ising: World's First Open-Source AI Model to Accelerate Practical Quantum Computer Progress

On April 14th, NVIDIA released its Ising open-source model series, providing world-leading artificial intelligence (AI)-based quantum processor calibration capabilities. Its quantum error correction decoding speed is up to 2.5 times faster and 3 times more accurate than traditional methods.

... Leading quantum companies, academic institutions, and research labs using the Ising model include: Academia Sinica, Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory, Harvard University's John Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences, Infleqtion, IQM Quantum Computers, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Advanced Quantum Testing Platform, and the UK's National Physical Laboratory (NPL).

▌CoW Swap front-end issues, users advised to temporarily refrain from using it

On April 14th, CoW DAO posted on the X platform that the CoW Swap front-end was experiencing issues and advised users to temporarily refrain from using it.

▌Kalshi teases new product launch on April 27th

On April 14th, the prediction platform Kalshi released a video teasing a new product launch scheduled for April 27th.

The video shows a green spiral forming a perpetual ring, eventually displaying the word "Timeless." The community speculates that this update may introduce a perpetual prediction market with no expiration date, breaking through the time limit of Kalshi's original event contracts and improving trading flexibility. According to market news, AI cloud platform FluidStack plans to raise approximately $1 billion at a valuation of $18 billion. Situational Awareness, an agency under Leopold Aschenbrenner, is in talks to participate and co-lead the round. This funding is expected to accelerate FluidStack's expansion in AI computing and cloud infrastructure, highlighting the trend of continued capital investment in the computing power sector. It is understood that FluidStack has previously signed cooperation agreements with several Bitcoin mining companies, including TeraWulf, Cipher Mining, and Hut 8. Cryptocurrency

According to Reuters, Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Warsh disclosed in a newly filed financial disclosure that he holds various future-oriented assets, including SpaceX and the prediction platform Polymarket. Warsh's main assets exceed $100 million, including two investments of over $50 million related to the Duquesne Family Office (managed by investor Stanley Druckenmiller). In the cryptocurrency and fintech sector, his portfolio also includes several related companies: Tenderly, an Ethereum developer platform; Stashfin, described as a "new bank for consumer loans"; and LemonCash, a platform providing crypto financial services.

Michael Saylor: Perpetual Preferred Stock STRC Achieves $278 Million in Daily Liquidity Michael Saylor, founder of Bitcoin treasury company Strategy, stated in an article on the X platform that his company's perpetual preferred stock STRC currently has a volatility of approximately 1.7%, a Sharpe ratio of 4.49, and daily liquidity of approximately $278 million, with overall performance approaching the stability level of money market products. He pointed out that STRC's volatility is already within the money market range, while other asset classes have not yet reached similar levels, and on this basis, STRC provides market-leading risk-adjusted returns. Paxos Labs Spins Off from Stablecoin Issuer Paxos and Raises $12 Million. Paxos Labs has spun off from stablecoin issuer Paxos and raised $12 million in funding. The round was led by Blockchain Capital, with participation from Robot Ventures, family office Maelstrom, and DeFi developer Uniswap Labs. Paxos Labs co-founder Bhaumik Kotecha declined to disclose the valuation of the round. Paxos CEO Chad Cascarilla is also the CEO of Paxos Labs. Paxos Labs will primarily provide services to large enterprises, enabling them to create and manage their own stablecoins.

▌Upbit Adds Zama (ZAMA) Listing

On April 14th, according to official news, Upbit added Zama (ZAMA) listing, supporting KRW, USDT, and BTC trading pairs.

Important Economic Developments

▌Bessant: Tariff Levels May Restore by Early July

On April 15th, US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that Trump's tariff levels may restore to levels before the Supreme Court overturned several imposition orders by early July. After the Supreme Court ruled that Trump's use of emergency powers to impose early tariffs was unconstitutional, Bessant is seeking to rebuild his "tariff wall" using different authorizations (such as Section 301 investigations).

Bessant noted that while it's difficult to say when the consequences of the war in Iran will "impact" the US economy, the current economic situation remains strong. He believes that the US growth rate this year could easily exceed 3% or even 3.5%. He added that the continued decline in core inflation, excluding volatile energy and food prices, is a good sign. Bessant stated, "I think the Fed has consistently misjudged inflation; core inflation is declining. If they want to wait for clearer data before taking action, I understand, but that would mean interest rates should be lowered further." (Jinshi)

▌Bessant: The Fed's wait-and-see approach to the development of the Iranian conflict is wise

April 14th, according to US financial media Semafor, US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated at the "Semafor World Economy" conference in Washington, D.C. on Monday that the Federal Reserve should "wait and see" before deciding whether to lower interest rates during the war in Iran.

Regarding whether interest rates should be lowered, Bessant said, "They will eventually be lowered. But I think we have to wait and see how the economy develops. But I think that going into January, and then into January and February—the economy performed very strongly." Bessant stated that the Fed's current approach of observing the development of the conflict in Iran is "wise." Bessant said, "If [the ECB] raises [interest rates], I would be shocked. But I would say that many European countries (like the UK) and Asian countries are subsidizing demand, while we in the US are not doing so." Bessant stated that he believes the recent price increases will not be "included in inflation expectations," and he believes that the current inflation is temporary. (Jinshi) A source familiar with internal discussions in Tehran revealed that Iran is considering temporarily suspending shipping through the Strait of Hormuz to avoid challenging the US maritime blockade and undermining a new round of peace talks. The source indicated that this potential suspension reflects Iran's desire to avoid an immediate escalation of the situation at a sensitive diplomatic moment as it prepares for logistical arrangements for the next face-to-face meeting with Washington and Tehran. The source stated that suspending maritime activity for several days is seen as a feasible and pragmatic measure to prevent events that could derail fragile efforts to resume negotiations. They added that Iran's considerations are still evolving. For example, the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps might quickly change tactics, attempting to demonstrate that the US blockade can be challenged without consequences, a move that could potentially disrupt the diplomatic track.

▌Senate Banking Committee Chairman: Warsh's Nomination Expected to be Confirmed

On April 14, according to Fox Business Network, Federal Reserve Chairman nominee Warsh has cleared the hurdles preventing his confirmation. Senator Tim Scott, Chairman of the Senate Banking Committee, stated that Warsh's hearing will be held next week. He expects Warsh's nomination to be confirmed. He is confident that the Justice Department will soon conclude its investigation into Powell.

▌Trump Says Talks with Iran "Possibly Within the Next Two Days" in Pakistan

On April 14, US President Trump told the New York Post that talks with Iran "possibly within the next two days" in Pakistan.

(Xinhua) On April 15, local time, US Vice President Vance expressed optimism about the current developments in the US-Iran situation. He also noted that significant distrust exists between the US and Iran, which cannot be resolved overnight. It was learned on the 14th that sources in the US indicated Vance is expected to lead a possible second round of US-Iran talks. Sources said that Trump's special envoy Witkov and his son-in-law Kushner will also participate. Trump has assigned the task of promoting a diplomatic solution to these three individuals and continues to maintain contact with Iran and intermediaries. Currently, the US is internally discussing arrangements for the second round of talks, but has not yet determined whether it will be held or the specific time. (CCTV) ▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in April is 98.4% ... Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.
